message:
perf(renderers): replace per-pixel set_pixel loops with slice fill for background drawing

Both the Doom and Quake renderers were calling set_pixel() in a tight inner loop
for every pixel in each background row. This is wasteful because the entire row
shares the same color value. Replace with direct slice::fill() on the framebuffer
pixel array, which lets the compiler emit a single memset-like operation per row
instead of N function calls with bounds checks.

- doom/renderer.rs: Pre-compute row_width, index directly into fb.pixels slice
- quake/renderer.rs: Same optimization applied to cached background fill

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
